title: Ordering — Discharge Letter to GP (Pneumonia)
brief: Drag the paragraphs into the order a GP would expect when receiving a discharge letter following a hospital admission for community-acquired pneumonia.

items.text: I am writing to update you on Mr Daniel Owens, a 71-year-old gentleman who was discharged from our care today following treatment for community-acquired pneumonia.

On admission, Mr Owens reported a five-day history of productive cough, fever, and increasing breathlessness. Examination revealed coarse crackles in the right base, oxygen saturation of 91% on air, and a CRP of 184 mg/L. A chest X-ray confirmed right lower-lobe consolidation, and he was admitted for intravenous antibiotic therapy.
His relevant background includes hypertension and well-controlled type 2 diabetes. He is a lifelong non-smoker, drinks alcohol within recommended limits, and lives independently with his wife.

Please review Mr Owens at one week to ensure ongoing clinical improvement and to repeat his FBC, U&E, and CRP. He has been discharged on oral amoxicillin to complete a seven-day course; antihypertensive and diabetic medications are unchanged.
Should you require any further information, please do not hesitate to contact me. Thank you for your continued care of Mr Owens.
